Home / Services / Ponds

Ponds

Custom pond construction for recreation, irrigation, livestock, and aesthetic value.

Get a Free Estimate

Overview

A well-built pond adds beauty, function, and value to any rural property. Whether you want a fishing pond, a livestock watering source, an irrigation reservoir, or a scenic feature that enhances your acreage, Earthwise Excavating has the experience to design and build it right.

We’ve been building ponds across Southwest Ohio for over two decades. Our knowledge of local soil conditions, hydrology, and Ohio’s regulatory landscape means your pond will hold water, function as intended, and enhance your land for years to come.

What’s Included

  • Site Assessment — We evaluate soil type, topography, watershed area, and groundwater conditions to determine the best pond location and design.
  • Design & Sizing — We work with you to determine the right size, depth, and shape based on your intended use and property characteristics.
  • Excavation — Our experienced operators dig the pond basin with proper depth profiles, shelves, and slopes for safety and function.
  • Dam & Spillway Construction — When applicable, we build properly compacted dams with engineered spillways to safely handle overflow.
  • Finish Grading & Seeding — We grade the banks, establish proper slopes, and seed disturbed areas for erosion control and a natural appearance.

Our Process

  1. Free Site Evaluation — We visit your property, test soil conditions, and discuss your vision for the pond.
  2. Design & Estimate — We present a design plan with dimensions, estimated fill time, and a clear cost breakdown.
  3. Expert Construction — Our crew excavates and shapes the pond with precision, building proper dams, spillways, and bank profiles.
  4. Restoration & Guidance — We restore the surrounding area with grading and seeding, and advise you on pond management for the first year.

FAQ

Common Questions About Ponds

How much does it cost to build a pond in Ohio? +

Pond costs vary significantly based on size, depth, soil conditions, and site accessibility. A typical small farm pond (quarter-acre) in Southwest Ohio generally ranges from $5,000 to $15,000, while larger ponds can cost more. Soil type is a major factor — clay-rich soils hold water naturally, while sandy or rocky sites may need a liner. We provide free on-site estimates with transparent pricing.

Will my property hold water for a pond? +

Southwest Ohio generally has favorable clay soils for pond construction, especially in Clinton, Highland, and Warren counties. During our site assessment, we evaluate soil composition, water table levels, and watershed area to determine if your site will support a pond. In many cases, the answer is yes.

Do I need a permit to build a pond in Ohio? +

Ohio requires permits for dams over a certain height or impoundment capacity. Most farm ponds fall below these thresholds, but it depends on the specific design. We'll help you determine permit requirements during the planning phase and can assist with the application process if needed.

How long does it take for a new pond to fill? +

Fill time depends on the pond size, watershed area, soil permeability, and rainfall. Most ponds in our area begin filling immediately from groundwater seepage and surface runoff. A typical half-acre pond in Southwest Ohio usually reaches full pool within 3-6 months, sometimes sooner with normal rainfall.

Ready to Get Started?

Contact us today for a free consultation about ponds.
